content: The act of human linguistic reflection (taught as a core vocabulary concept in high school English literacy) can be formalized as a structured prompt template to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where learners are trained to explicitly frame reflections around what they got wrong, why, and how to adjust—exactly the structured self-critique missing from many current LLM reflection schemes. This turns a mundane language learning concept into a blueprint for more effective autonomous agent self-reflection.
